name: Spinach Lasagna
recipeIngredient: 1 pound other cheese (cheddar, swiss, jack, etc.)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sauce: Combine tomatoes and tomato sauce in a large pot over low heat. Add garlic. Chop onions, olives, mushrooms, and broccoli; add them as well. Incorporate the spices to taste. Simmer covered over low heat until all the tomatoes have melded with the rest of the sauce.
2. Cheese: Grate the cheeses and mix thoroughly. Add eggs, parsley, and spinach, mixing well.
3. Building the lasagna: In a lasagna pan, spread enough sauce on the bottom to cover it. Layer noodles over the sauce. Using about half of the cheese mixture, sprinkle cheese over the noodles. Add another layer of noodles, then more sauce to ensure all noodles are coated. Continue alternating layers of noodles and sauce until finished. Use any remaining cheese mixture for the final layer of cheese.
4. Bake at 190°C (374°F) for 45 minutes.
Author's Notes:
This recipe was shared on usenet by sridhar@spt.tek.com, who had originally received it from Sean Gilley (slg@e.ms.uky.edu). I made slight adjustments to suit my preferences.
The lasagna noodles are the layers you assemble. Be cautious with the sauce; if used liberally, it may run out before finishing. Some prefer a different layering approach: (c-n-s-c-n-s-c-n-s), where c represents cheese, n is noodles, and s is sauce.
